---
name: module-scaffolding
description: >
  Step-by-step guide for adding a new module to AuctionService (ASP.NET Core 10 / C# 13).
  Use when creating a new module, adding a new bounded context, or setting up a new
  Domain/Application/Infrastructure layer from scratch.
applyTo: "src/Modules/**"
---

# Module Scaffolding — AuctionService

Every module lives under `src/Modules/<ModuleName>/` and follows the same structure.
Use this guide whenever you create a new bounded context (e.g., `Ordering`, `Notification`).

---

## 1. Target Folder Structure

```
src/Modules/<ModuleName>/
├── <ModuleName>.csproj
├── Application/
│   ├── DependencyInjection.cs          # Module DI registration
│   ├── Commands/
│   │   └── <Action><ModuleName>/
│   │       ├── <Action><ModuleName>Command.cs
│   │       ├── <Action><ModuleName>CommandHandler.cs
│   │       └── <Action><ModuleName>CommandValidator.cs
│   └── Queries/
│       └── Get<ModuleName>ById/
│           ├── Get<ModuleName>ByIdQuery.cs
│           └── Get<ModuleName>ByIdQueryHandler.cs
├── Domain/
│   └── Entities/
│       └── <ModuleName>Entity.cs
└── Infrastructure/
    └── Persistence/
        ├── <ModuleName>DbContext.cs
        └── Migrations/                 # Generated by EF Core CLI
```

---

## 2. Step-by-Step Checklist

### Step 1 — Create the .csproj

```xml
<!-- src/Modules/<ModuleName>/<ModuleName>.csproj -->
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <ItemGroup>
    <ProjectReference Include="..\..\AuctionService.Shared\AuctionService.Shared.csproj" />
  </ItemGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="FluentValidation" Version="12.0.0" />
    <PackageReference Include="MediatR" Version="12.4.1" />
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore" Version="10.0.0" />
    <PackageReference Include="Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL" Version="10.0.0" />
  </ItemGroup>

  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net10.0</TargetFramework>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
  </PropertyGroup>

</Project>
```

### Step 2 — Add project reference to AuctionService.Api

In `src/AuctionService.Api/AuctionService.Api.csproj`:
```xml
<ProjectReference Include="..\Modules\<ModuleName>\<ModuleName>.csproj" />
```

### Step 3 — Create the Domain Entity

```csharp
// Domain/Entities/<ModuleName>Entity.cs
using AuctionService.Shared;

namespace <ModuleName>.Domain.Entities;

public class <ModuleName>Entity : BaseEntity
{
    // Properties with private setters
    public string Name { get; private set; } = string.Empty;

    private <ModuleName>Entity() { }   // EF Core needs parameterless ctor

    public static <ModuleName>Entity Create(string name)
    {
        ArgumentException.ThrowIfNullOrWhiteSpace(name);

        var now = DateTimeOffset.UtcNow;
        return new <ModuleName>Entity
        {
            Name = name.Trim(),
            CreatedAt = now,
            UpdatedAt = now
        };
    }
}
```

### Step 4 — Create the DbContext

```csharp
// Infrastructure/Persistence/<ModuleName>DbContext.cs
using <ModuleName>.Domain.Entities;
using Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore;

namespace <ModuleName>.Infrastructure.Persistence;

public class <ModuleName>DbContext : DbContext
{
    public <ModuleName>DbContext(DbContextOptions<<ModuleName>DbContext> options) : base(options) { }

    public DbSet<<ModuleName>Entity> Items => Set<<ModuleName>Entity>();

    protected override void OnModelCreating(ModelBuilder modelBuilder)
    {
        modelBuilder.HasDefaultSchema("<modulename>");   // lowercase schema name

        modelBuilder.Entity<<ModuleName>Entity>(builder =>
        {
            builder.ToTable("<table_name>");             // lowercase snake_case
            builder.HasKey(x => x.Id);
            builder.Property(x => x.Name).HasMaxLength(200).IsRequired();
        });
    }
}
```

**Schema naming rule:** schema = lowercase module name (e.g., `member`, `auction`, `bidding`).

### Step 5 — Create DependencyInjection

```csharp
// Application/DependencyInjection.cs
using <ModuleName>.Infrastructure.Persistence;
using MediatR;
using Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore;
using Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration;
using Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection;

namespace <ModuleName>;

public static class <ModuleName>DependencyInjection
{
    public static IServiceCollection Add<ModuleName>Module(
        this IServiceCollection services,
        IConfiguration configuration)
    {
        var connectionString = configuration.GetConnectionString("<ModuleName>Connection")
            ?? configuration.GetConnectionString("DefaultConnection")
            ?? throw new InvalidOperationException(
                "Connection string for <ModuleName> module is not configured.");

        services.AddDbContext<<ModuleName>DbContext>(options =>
            options.UseNpgsql(connectionString));

        services.AddMediatR(cfg =>
            cfg.RegisterServicesFromAssembly(typeof(<ModuleName>DependencyInjection).Assembly));

        return services;
    }
}
```

### Step 6 — Register in Program.cs

```csharp
// src/AuctionService.Api/Program.cs
using <ModuleName>;
using <ModuleName>.Infrastructure.Persistence;

// Add after existing module registrations:
builder.Services.Add<ModuleName>Module(builder.Configuration);
```

### Step 7 — Add Migration

```bash
dotnet ef migrations add InitialCreate \
  --project src/Modules/<ModuleName>/<ModuleName>.csproj \
  --startup-project src/AuctionService.Api/AuctionService.Api.csproj \
  --output-dir Infrastructure/Persistence/Migrations
```

### Step 8 — Apply Migration

```bash
dotnet ef database update \
  --project src/Modules/<ModuleName>/<ModuleName>.csproj \
  --startup-project src/AuctionService.Api/AuctionService.Api.csproj
```

---

## 3. Module Isolation Rules

| Rule | Reason |
|------|--------|
| No cross-module DbContext | Each module owns its data; no `AuctionDbContext` inside `BiddingDbContext` |
| Use `Guid` for cross-module references | Logical FK, never EF Core navigation property across modules |
| Each module has its own schema | `HasDefaultSchema("<modulename>")` in `OnModelCreating` |
| No shared Entity classes | Never reference `Member.Domain.Entities.MemberUser` from Auction module |
| Module namespace = module name | `namespace Auction.Application.Commands.CreateAuction` |

---

## 4. Completed Module Reference

| Module | Schema | DbContext | DI method |
|--------|--------|-----------|-----------|
| Member | `member` | `MemberDbContext` | `AddMemberModule` |
| Auction | `auction` | `AuctionDbContext` | `AddAuctionModule` |
| Bidding | `bidding` | `BiddingDbContext` | `AddBiddingModule` |
| Ordering | `ordering` | `OrderingDbContext` | `AddOrderingModule` |
| Notification | `notification` | `NotificationDbContext` | `AddNotificationModule` |

---

## 5. New Module Checklist

- [ ] `.csproj` created with correct package references + `ProjectReference` to Shared
- [ ] Api `.csproj` has `ProjectReference` to new module
- [ ] Domain entity inherits `BaseEntity`, has `private` parameterless ctor, static `Create()` factory
- [ ] DbContext has `HasDefaultSchema("<modulename>")` (lowercase)
- [ ] `DependencyInjection.cs` throws on missing connection string
- [ ] `Program.cs` calls `Add<ModuleName>Module(builder.Configuration)`
- [ ] EF Core migration created and applied
- [ ] `dotnet build` passes with zero errors
- [ ] At least one unit test for the domain entity
